primary.title: JSON Formatter & Validator - Beautify JSON Online
primary.description: Format, beautify, validate, and minify raw JSON string content with our free online developer utility. Direct syntax error reporting.
primary.heading: JSON Formatter & Validator
primary.seo_content:
JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) is a lightweight, language-independent data-interchange format that is easy for humans to read and write and easy for machines to parse and generate. JSON is widely used in API responses, system configuration files, and web databases.

Minified or poorly structured JSON is tough to debug. Our client-side JSON Formatter parses your input, highlights syntax anomalies, and formats the data with clean indentations to make it immediately readable.

examples.title: Beautifying API logs
examples.description: Paste a raw, single-line API response like '{"user":"SatX","id":12}' to format it into a multi-line, indented JSON block.

examples.title: Detecting broken commas
examples.description: If your JSON is missing a comma or has a trailing comma, the parser will catch the exception and display a warning indicating the exact syntax position.

faqs.question: What is JSON validation?
faqs.answer: JSON validation is the process of checking if a text string matches the official JSON specifications (RFC 8259). This includes verifying quote marks around keys, matching brackets, and proper commas.

faqs.question: Does my formatted JSON get uploaded?
faqs.answer: No. Like all SatX Tools, the JSON Formatter processes your code entirely in your local web browser. Your configuration files and private data never travel over the network or touch any servers, making it 100% secure.

json-validator.title: JSON Validator - Validate and Debug JSON Online
json-validator.description: Verify your JSON structure. Catch parsing errors, trailing commas, and unclosed quotes with our free online validator.
json-validator.heading: JSON Validator
json-validator.seo_content:
A JSON Validator is a syntax debugging utility for developers and system administrators. When editing complex server configurations, app manifests, or REST API payloads, tiny syntax mistakes can crash applications.

This tool validates raw text blocks, identifying missing commas, mismatched braces, or double-quotes, allowing you to debug and correct payloads instantly.

examples.title: Locating a trailing comma error
examples.description: Pasting '[1, 2, 3,]' into the validator reports a syntax error because standard JSON forbids trailing commas inside arrays.

examples.title: Verifying nested objects
examples.description: Paste multi-layered database payloads to confirm all brackets close correctly.

faqs.question: Why does standard JSON require double quotes?
faqs.answer: The official JSON specification requires double quotes (") around keys and string values. Single quotes (') will cause a syntax validation error.

faqs.question: How can I fix a 'JSON parsing error'?
faqs.answer: Review the error message provided by our validator. It indicates the character position where parsing failed. Check for missing commas, unclosed brackets, or unescaped characters around that line.

json-beautifier.title: JSON Beautifier - Format and Clean Up JSON Code
json-beautifier.description: Beautify raw, minified JSON into human-readable formatted code. Free online developer formatting tool.
json-beautifier.heading: JSON Beautifier
json-beautifier.seo_content:
A JSON Beautifier translates raw, single-line JSON data into a beautifully spaced, human-readable structure. Minified payloads are ideal for network speeds but impossible to inspect visually. This tool cleans up the code in a single click.

Select your spacing size (2 or 4 spaces) and beautify the code instantly.

examples.title: Beautifying minified logs
examples.description: Pasting a compressed database record block formats it with clear hierarchy indentations.

examples.title: Adjusting indentation
examples.description: Toggle between 2-space indentation (compact) and 4-space indentation (standard) to match your team coding guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the difference between beautifying and minifying?
faqs.answer: Beautifying adds line breaks and space indentations to make code readable. Minifying strips out all whitespace, tabs, and line breaks to minimize file size.

faqs.question: Can I minify my JSON here as well?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our tool supports both beautification (formatting) and minification, allowing you to compress JSON payloads for optimized server transmissions.
